content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                    Date : January 10, 1978


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Barbara Campbell [signature]

Subject: Filing Pursuant to Government Code Section
         81005(b)


       At the January 4, 1978, Opinion Request meeting,
staff concluded that a controlled committee of a candidate
specified in Government Code Section 81005(b) may choose
to file, under that section, two copies of its campaign
statements with either the clerk of the county in which
the candidate resides or the clerk of the county in
which the committee is domiciled. The committee must
also file the original and one copy with the Secretary
of State and two copies with the clerk of the largest
county which in whole or in part is included in the
candidate's election district.

       However, if a candidate, specified in Government
Code Section 81005(b), and her/his controlled committee
filed a consolidated campaign statement, under Government
Code Section 84213, that choice would not be available
to them. Two copies of a consolidated campaign statement
must be filed with the clerk of the county in which the
candidate resides and the clerk of the county in which
the committee is domiciled. The consolidated campaign
statement must be filed in as many locations as necessary
to satisfy the obligations of both the candidate and
the committee.


BC:plh
cc: Gina Kutchins
    Ted Prim
    Lee C. Rosenthal

sections.question_synthetic: Where must a controlled committee of a candidate specified in Government Code Section 81005(b) file campaign statements, and does this differ when filing a consolidated campaign statement?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A controlled committee may file two copies of campaign statements with either the county clerk where the candidate resides or where the committee is domiciled, plus copies with the Secretary of State and the largest county in the election district. However, consolidated campaign statements must be filed with both the candidate's and committee's county clerks in all necessary locations to satisfy both parties' obligations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; content extracted from staff conclusion paragraph. Document appears to be internal staff memo from January 4, 1978 Opinion Request meeting. Filing requirements are primary substantive content.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Where must a controlled committee of a candidate specified in Government Code Section 81005(b) file campaign statements, and does this differ when filing a consolidated campaign statement?

CONCLUSION: A controlled committee may file two copies of campaign statements with either the county clerk where the candidate resides or where the committee is domiciled, plus copies with the Secretary of State and the largest county in the election district. However, consolidated campaign statements must be filed with both the candidate's and committee's county clerks in all necessary locations to satisfy both parties' obligations.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To : File Date : January 10, 1978 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Barbara Campbell [signature] Subject: Filing Pursuant to Government Code Section 81005(b) At the January 4, 1978, Opinion Request meeting, staff concluded that a controlled committee of a candidate specified in Government Code Section 81005(b) may choose to file, under that section, two copies of its campaign statements with either the clerk of the county in which the candidate resides or the clerk of the county in which the committee is domiciled. The committee must also file the original and one copy with the Secretary of State and two copies with the clerk of the largest county which in whole or in part is included in the candidate's election district. However, if a candidate, specified in Government Code Section 81005(b), and her/his controlled committee filed a consolidated campaign statement, under Government Code Section 84213, that choice would not be available to them. Two copies of a consolidated campaign statement must be filed with the clerk of the county in which the candidate resides and the clerk of the county in which the committee is domiciled. The consolidated campaign statement must be filed in as many locations as necessary to satisfy the obligations of both the candidate and the committee. BC:plh cc: Gina Kutchins Ted Prim Lee C. Rosenthal
embedding.summary: FPPC staff concluded on filing requirements for controlled committees under Government Code Section 81005(b), specifying different filing locations for regular versus consolidated campaign statements.
